children.text: I went to the National Museum of China yesterday. I saw an ancient dumpling from Xinjiang!
children.cn: 我昨天去了中国国家博物馆。我看到了一个来自新疆的古代饺子！
children.statement: 作者在博物馆看到了一个现代的饺子。
children.explanation: 文中说看到的是 'ancient dumpling'（古代饺子）。

children.text: It looks just like dumplings today. What did ancient people put in it? No one knows for sure.
children.cn: 它看起来和今天的饺子一模一样。古人在里面放了什么？没有人确切知道。
children.statement: 这个古代饺子的外观和现在的饺子非常不同。
children.explanation: 文中说 'It looks just like dumplings today'。

children.text: The dumpling is from the Tang Dynasty. People ate dumplings over 1,000 years ago!
children.cn: 这个饺子来自唐朝。人们在1000多年前就在吃饺子了！
children.explanation: 文中提到饺子来自唐朝，人们在1000多年前吃它。

children.text: At that time, Xinjiang was an important place in China.
children.cn: 在那时，新疆是中国一个非常重要的地方。

children.text: People in central China went there by the ancient Silk Road. They brought dumplings to Xinjiang.
children.cn: 中原地区的人们通过古代丝绸之路去那里。他们把饺子带到了新疆。
children.statement: 饺子是通过丝绸之路传到新疆的。
children.explanation: 文中提到中原人通过丝绸之路把饺子带到了新疆。

children.text: People ate dumplings so long ago! How interesting!
children.cn: 人们在那么久以前就吃饺子了！真有意思！
